NKJV.1: After these things Jesus went over the Sea of Galilee, which is the Sea of Tiberias.
NKJV.2: Then a great multitude followed Him, because they saw His signs which He performed on those who were diseased.
NKJV.3: And Jesus went up on the mountain, and there He sat with His disciples.
NKJV.4: Now the Passover, a feast of the Jews, was near.
NKJV.5: Then Jesus lifted up His eyes, and seeing a great multitude coming toward Him, He said to Philip, “Where shall we buy bread, that these may eat?”
NKJV.6: But this He said to test him, for He Himself knew what He would do.
NKJV.7: Philip answered Him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, that every one of them may have a little.”

NKJV.9: “There is a lad here who has five barley loaves and two small fish, but what are they among so many?”
NKJV.10: Then Jesus said, “Make the people sit down.” Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
NKJV.11: And Jesus took the loaves, and when He had given thanks He distributed them to the disciples, and the disciples to those sitting down; and likewise of the fish, as much as they wanted.
NKJV.12: So when they were filled, He said to His disciples, “Gather up the fragments that remain, so that nothing is lost.”
NKJV.13: Therefore they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with the fragments of the five barley loaves which were left over by those who had eaten.
NKJV.14: Then those men, when they had seen the sign that Jesus did, said, “This is truly the Prophet who is to come into the world.”
NKJV.15: Therefore when Jesus perceived that they were about to come and take Him by force to make Him king, He departed again to the mountain by Himself alone.
NKJV.16: Now when evening came, His disciples went down to the sea,
NKJV.17: got into the boat, and went over the sea toward Capernaum. And it was already dark, and Jesus had not come to them.
NKJV.18: Then the sea arose because a great wind was blowing.
NKJV.19: So when they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and drawing near the boat; and they were afraid.
NKJV.20: But He said to them, “It is I; do not be afraid.”
NKJV.21: Then they willingly received Him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at the land where they were going.
NKJV.22: On the following day, when the people who were standing on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other boat there, except that one which His disciples had entered, and that Jesus had not entered the boat with His disciples, but His disciples had gone away alone—
NKJV.23: however, other boats came from Tiberias, near the place where they ate bread after the Lord had given thanks—
NKJV.24: when the people therefore saw that Jesus was not there, nor His disciples, they also got into boats and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.
NKJV.25: And when they found Him on the other side of the sea, they said to Him, “Rabbi, when did You come here?”
NKJV.26: Jesus answered them and said, “Most assuredly, I say to you, you seek Me, not because you saw the signs, but because you ate of the loaves and were filled.
NKJV.27: Do not labor for the food which perishes, but  for the food which endures to everlasting life, which the Son of Man will give you,  because God the Father has set His seal on Him.”
NKJV.28: Then they said to Him, “What shall we do, that we may work the works of God?”
NKJV.29: Jesus answered and said to them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in Him whom He sent.”
NKJV.30: Therefore they said to Him, “What sign will You perform then, that we may see it and believe You? What work will You do?

NKJV.32: Then Jesus said to them, “Most assuredly, I say to you, Moses did not give you the bread from heaven, but  My Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
NKJV.33: For the bread of God is He who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
NKJV.34: Then they said to Him, “Lord, give us this bread always.”
NKJV.35: And Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life.  He who comes to Me shall never hunger, and he who believes in Me shall never  thirst.
NKJV.36: But I said to you that you have seen Me and yet  do not believe.
NKJV.37: All that the Father gives Me will come to Me, and  the one who comes to Me I will by no means cast out.
NKJV.38: For I have come down from heaven,  not to do My own will,  but the will of Him who sent Me.
NKJV.39: This is the will of the Father who sent Me,  that of all He has given Me I should lose nothing, but should raise it up at the last day.
NKJV.40: And this is the will of Him who sent Me,  that everyone who sees the Son and believes in Him may have everlasting life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”

NKJV.42: And they said, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How is it then that He says, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”

NKJV.44: No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me  draws him; and I will raise him up at the last day.

NKJV.48: I am the bread of life.

ESV.1: After this Jesus went away to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, which is the Sea of Tiberias.
ESV.2: And a large crowd was following him, because they saw the signs that he was doing on the sick.
ESV.3: Jesus went up on the mountain, and there he sat down with his disciples.
ESV.4: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was at hand.
ESV.5: Lifting up his eyes, then, and seeing that a large crowd was coming toward him, Jesus said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread, so that these people may eat?”
ESV.6: He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he would do.
ESV.7: Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii  worth of bread would not be enough for each of them to get a little.”
ESV.8: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, said to him,
ESV.9: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are they for so many?”
ESV.10: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, about five thousand in number.
ESV.11: Jesus then took the loaves, and when he had given thanks, he distributed them to those who were seated. So also the fish, as much as they wanted.
ESV.12: And when they had eaten their fill, he told his disciples, “Gather up the leftover fragments, that nothing may be lost.”
ESV.13: So they gathered them up and filled twelve baskets with fragments from the five barley loaves left by those who had eaten.
ESV.14: When the people saw the sign that he had done, they said, “This is indeed the Prophet who is to come into the world!”
ESV.15: Perceiving then that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, Jesus withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
ESV.16: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
ESV.17: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
ESV.18: The sea became rough because a strong wind was blowing.
ESV.19: When they had rowed about three or four miles,  they saw Jesus walking on the sea and coming near the boat, and they were frightened.
ESV.20: But he said to them, “It is I; do not be afraid.”
ESV.21: Then they were glad to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at the land to which they were going.
ESV.22: On the next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the sea saw that there had been only one boat there, and that Jesus had not entered the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
ESV.23: Other boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
ESV.24: So when the crowd saw that Jesus was not there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats and went to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.
ESV.25: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
ESV.26: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, you are seeking me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves.
ESV.27: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For on him God the Father has set his seal.”
ESV.28: Then they said to him, “What must we do, to be doing the works of God?”
ESV.29: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
ESV.30: So they said to him, “Then what sign do you do, that we may see and believe you? What work do you perform?
ESV.31: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
ESV.32: Jesus then said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.
ESV.33: For the bread of God is he who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
ESV.34: They said to him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
ESV.35: Jesus said to them,  “I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me shall not hunger, and whoever believes in me shall never thirst.
ESV.36: But I said to you that you have seen me and yet do not believe.
ESV.37: All that the Father gives me will come to me, and whoever comes to me I will never cast out.
ESV.38: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will but the will of him who sent me.
ESV.39: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I should lose nothing of all that he has given me, but raise it up on the last day.
ESV.40: For this is the will of my Father, that everyone who looks on the Son and believes in him should have eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.”
ESV.41: So the Jews grumbled about him, because he said,  “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”
ESV.42: They said, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How does he now say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
ESV.43: Jesus answered them, “Do not grumble among yourselves.
ESV.44: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him. And I will raise him up on the last day.
ESV.45: It is written in the Prophets, ‘And they will all be taught by God.’ Everyone who has heard and learned from the Father comes to me —
ESV.46: not that anyone has seen the Father except he who is from God; he has seen the Father.
ESV.47: Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever believes has eternal life.
ESV.48: I am the bread of life.
ESV.49: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
ESV.50: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
ESV.51: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. And the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
ESV.52: The Jews then disputed among themselves, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
ESV.53: So Jesus said to them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
ESV.54: Whoever feeds on my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.
ESV.55: For my flesh is true food, and my blood is true drink.
ESV.56: Whoever feeds on my flesh and drinks my blood abides in me, and I in him.
ESV.57: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever feeds on me, he also will live because of me.
ESV.58: This is the bread that came down from heaven, not like the bread  the fathers ate, and died. Whoever feeds on this bread will live forever.”
ESV.59: Jesus  said these things in the synagogue, as he taught at Capernaum.
ESV.60: When many of his disciples heard it, they said, “This is a hard saying; who can listen to it?”
ESV.61: But Jesus, knowing in himself that his disciples were grumbling about this, said to them, “Do you take offense at this?
ESV.62: Then what if you were to see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
ESV.63: It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh is no help at all. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
ESV.64: But there are some of you who do not believe.” (For Jesus knew from the beginning who those were who did not believe, and who it was who would betray him.)
ESV.65: And he said, “This is why I told you that no one can come to me unless it is granted him by the Father.”
ESV.66: After this many of his disciples turned back and no longer walked with him.
ESV.67: So Jesus said to the twelve, “Do you want to go away as well?”
ESV.68: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life,
ESV.69: and we have believed, and have come to know, that you are the Holy One of God.”
ESV.70: Jesus answered them,  “Did I not choose you, the twelve? And yet one of you is a devil.”
ESV.71: He spoke of Judas the son of Simon Iscariot, for he, one of the twelve, was going to betray him.

NASB.1: After these things Jesus went away to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (or Tiberias).
NASB.2: A large crowd followed Him, because they saw the signs which He was performing on those who were sick.
NASB.3: Then Jesus went up on the mountain, and there He sat down with His disciples.
NASB.4: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was near.
NASB.5: Therefore Jesus, lifting up His eyes and seeing that a large crowd was coming to Him, said to Philip, 'Where are we to buy bread, so that these may eat?'
NASB.6: This He was saying to test him, for He Himself knew what He was intending to do.
NASB.7: Philip answered Him, 'Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, for everyone to receive a little.'
NASB.8: One of His disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, said to Him,
NASB.9: 'There is a lad here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these for so many people?'
NASB.10: Jesus said, 'Have the people sit down.' Now there was much grass in the place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
NASB.11: Jesus then took the loaves, and having given thanks, He distributed to those who were seated; likewise also of the fish as much as they wanted.
NASB.12: When they were filled, He said to His disciples, 'Gather up the leftover fragments so that nothing will be lost.'
NASB.13: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with fragments from the five barley loaves which were left over by those who had eaten.
NASB.14: Therefore when the people saw the sign which He had performed, they said, 'This is truly the Prophet who is to come into the world.'
NASB.15: So Jesus, perceiving that they were intending to come and take Him by force to make Him king, withdrew again to the mountain by Himself alone.
NASB.16: Now when evening came, His disciples went down to the sea,
NASB.17: and after getting into a boat, they [started to] cross the sea to Capernaum. It had already become dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
NASB.18: The sea [began] to be stirred up because a strong wind was blowing.
NASB.19: Then, when they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and drawing near to the boat; and they were frightened.
NASB.20: But He said to them, 'It is I; do not be afraid.'
NASB.21: So they were willing to receive Him into the boat, and immediately the boat was at the land to which they were going.
NASB.22: The next day the crowd that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other small boat there, except one, and that Jesus had not entered with His disciples into the boat, but [that] His disciples had gone away alone.
NASB.23: There came other small boats from Tiberias near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
NASB.24: So when the crowd saw that Jesus was not there, nor His disciples, they themselves got into the small boats, and came to Capernaum seeking Jesus.
NASB.25: When they found Him on the other side of the sea, they said to Him, 'Rabbi, when did You get here?'
NASB.26: Jesus answered them and said, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, you seek Me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves and were filled.
NASB.27: 'Do not work for the food which perishes, but for the food which endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you, for on Him the Father, God, has set His seal.'
NASB.28: Therefore they said to Him, 'What shall we do, so that we may work the works of God?'
NASB.29: Jesus answered and said to them, 'This is the work of God, that you believe in Him whom He has sent.'
NASB.30: So they said to Him, 'What then do You do for a sign, so that we may see, and believe You? What work do You perform?
NASB.31: 'Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, 'HE GAVE THEM BREAD OUT OF HEAVEN TO EAT.''
NASB.32: Jesus then said to them, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, it is not Moses who has given you the bread out of heaven, but it is My Father who gives you the true bread out of heaven.
NASB.33: 'For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world.'
NASB.34: Then they said to Him, 'Lord, always give us this bread.'
NASB.35: Jesus said to them, 'I am the bread of life; he who comes to Me will not hunger, and he who believes in Me will never thirst.
NASB.36: 'But I said to you that you have seen Me, and yet do not believe.
NASB.37: 'All that the Father gives Me will come to Me, and the one who comes to Me I will certainly not cast out.
NASB.38: 'For I have come down from heaven, not to do My own will, but the will of Him who sent Me.
NASB.39: 'This is the will of Him who sent Me, that of all that He has given Me I lose nothing, but raise it up on the last day.
NASB.40: 'For this is the will of My Father, that everyone who beholds the Son and believes in Him will have eternal life, and I Myself will raise him up on the last day.'
NASB.41: Therefore the Jews were grumbling about Him, because He said, 'I am the bread that came down out of heaven.'
NASB.42: They were saying, 'Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How does He now say, 'I have come down out of heaven '?'
NASB.43: Jesus answered and said to them, 'Do not grumble among yourselves.
NASB.44: 'No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me draws him; and I will raise him up on the last day.
NASB.45: 'It is written in the prophets, 'AND THEY SHALL ALL BE TAUGHT OF GOD.' Everyone who has heard and learned from the Father, comes to Me.
NASB.46: 'Not that anyone has seen the Father, except the One who is from God; He has seen the Father.
NASB.47: 'Truly, truly, I say to you, he who believes has eternal life.
NASB.49: 'Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
NASB.50: 'This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
NASB.51: 'I am the living bread that came down out of heaven; if anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever; and the bread also which I will give for the life of the world is My flesh.'
NASB.52: Then the Jews [began] to argue with one another, saying, 'How can this man give us [His] flesh to eat?'
NASB.53: So Jesus said to them, 'Truly, truly, I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink His blood, you have no life in yourselves.
NASB.54: 'He who eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.
NASB.55: 'For My flesh is true food, and My blood is true drink.
NASB.56: 'He who eats My flesh and drinks My blood abides in Me, and I in him.
NASB.57: 'As the living Father sent Me, and I live because of the Father, so he who eats Me, he also will live because of Me.
NASB.58: 'This is the bread which came down out of heaven; not as the fathers ate and died; he who eats this bread will live forever.'
NASB.59: These things He said in the synagogue as He taught in Capernaum.
NASB.60: Therefore many of His disciples, when they heard [this] said, 'This is a difficult statement; who can listen to it?'
NASB.61: But Jesus, conscious that His disciples grumbled at this, said to them, 'Does this cause you to stumble?
NASB.62: '[What] then if you see the Son of Man ascending to where He was before?
NASB.63: 'It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh profits nothing; the words that I have spoken to you are spirit and are life.
NASB.64: 'But there are some of you who do not believe.' For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who did not believe, and who it was that would betray Him.
NASB.65: And He was saying, 'For this reason I have said to you, that no one can come to Me unless it has been granted him from the Father.'
NASB.66: As a result of this many of His disciples withdrew and were not walking with Him anymore.
NASB.67: So Jesus said to the twelve, 'You do not want to go away also, do you?'
NASB.68: Simon Peter answered Him, 'Lord, to whom shall we go? You have words of eternal life.
NASB.69: 'We have believed and have come to know that You are the Holy One of God.'
NASB.70: Jesus answered them, 'Did I Myself not choose you, the twelve, and [yet] one of you is a devil?'
NASB.71: Now He meant Judas [the son] of Simon Iscariot, for he, one of the twelve, was going to betray Him.

NLT.1: After this, Jesus crossed over to the far side of the Sea of Galilee, also known as the Sea of Tiberias.
NLT.2: A huge crowd kept following him wherever he went, because they saw his miraculous signs as he healed the sick.
NLT.3: Then Jesus climbed a hill and sat down with his disciples around him.
NLT.4: (It was nearly time for the Jewish Passover celebration.)
NLT.5: Jesus soon saw a huge crowd of people coming to look for him. Turning to Philip, he asked, “Where can we buy bread to feed all these people?”
NLT.6: He was testing Philip, for he already knew what he was going to do.
NLT.7: Philip replied, “Even if we worked for months, we wouldn’t have enough money to feed them!”
NLT.8: Then Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, spoke up.
NLT.9: “There’s a young boy here with five barley loaves and two fish. But what good is that with this huge crowd?”
NLT.10: “Tell everyone to sit down,” Jesus said. So they all sat down on the grassy slopes. (The men alone numbered about 5,000.)
NLT.11: Then Jesus took the loaves, gave thanks to God, and distributed them to the people. Afterward he did the same with the fish. And they all ate as much as they wanted.
NLT.12: After everyone was full, Jesus told his disciples, “Now gather the leftovers, so that nothing is wasted.”
NLT.13: So they picked up the pieces and filled twelve baskets with scraps left by the people who had eaten from the five barley loaves.
NLT.14: When the people saw him do this miraculous sign, they exclaimed, “Surely, he is the Prophet we have been expecting!”
NLT.15: When Jesus saw that they were ready to force him to be their king, he slipped away into the hills by himself.
NLT.16: That evening Jesus’ disciples went down to the shore to wait for him.
NLT.17: But as darkness fell and Jesus still hadn’t come back, they got into the boat and headed across the lake toward Capernaum.
NLT.18: Soon a gale swept down upon them, and the sea grew very rough.
NLT.19: They had rowed three or four miles when suddenly they saw Jesus walking on the water toward the boat. They were terrified,
NLT.20: but he called out to them, “Don’t be afraid. I am here! ”
NLT.21: Then they were eager to let him in the boat, and immediately they arrived at their destination!
NLT.22: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the far shore saw that the disciples had taken the only boat, and they realized Jesus had not gone with them.
NLT.23: Several boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the Lord had blessed the bread and the people had eaten.
NLT.24: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went across to Capernaum to look for him.
NLT.25: They found him on the other side of the lake and asked, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
NLT.26: Jesus replied, “I tell you the truth, you want to be with me because I fed you, not because you understood the miraculous signs.
NLT.27: But don’t be so concerned about perishable things like food. Spend your energy seeking the eternal life that the Son of Man can give you. For God the Father has given me the seal of his approval.”
NLT.28: They replied, “We want to perform God’s works, too. What should we do?”
NLT.29: Jesus told them, “This is the only work God wants from you: Believe in the one he has sent.”
NLT.30: They answered, “Show us a miraculous sign if you want us to believe in you. What can you do?
NLT.31: After all, our ancestors ate manna while they journeyed through the wilderness! The Scriptures say, ‘Moses gave them bread from heaven to eat.’ ”
NLT.32: Jesus said, “I tell you the truth, Moses didn’t give you bread from heaven. My Father did. And now he offers you the true bread from heaven.
NLT.33: The true bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
NLT.34: “Sir,” they said, “give us that bread every day.”
NLT.35: Jesus replied, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never be hungry again. Whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
NLT.36: But you haven’t believed in me even though you have seen me.
NLT.37: However, those the Father has given me will come to me, and I will never reject them.
NLT.38: For I have come down from heaven to do the will of God who sent me, not to do my own will.
NLT.39: And this is the will of God, that I should not lose even one of all those he has given me, but that I should raise them up at the last day.
NLT.40: For it is my Father’s will that all who see his Son and believe in him should have eternal life. I will raise them up at the last day.”
NLT.41: Then the people began to murmur in disagreement because he had said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”
NLT.42: They said, “Isn’t this Jesus, the son of Joseph? We know his father and mother. How can he say, ‘I came down from heaven’?”
NLT.43: But Jesus replied, “Stop complaining about what I said.
NLT.44: For no one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws them to me, and at the last day I will raise them up.
NLT.45: As it is written in the Scriptures, ‘They will all be taught by God.’ Everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him comes to me.
NLT.46: (Not that anyone has ever seen the Father; only I, who was sent from God, have seen him.)
NLT.47: “I tell you the truth, anyone who believes has eternal life.
NLT.48: Yes, I am the bread of life!
NLT.49: Your ancestors ate manna in the wilderness, but they all died.
NLT.50: Anyone who eats the bread from heaven, however, will never die.
NLT.51: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Anyone who eats this bread will live forever; and this bread, which I will offer so the world may live, is my flesh.”
NLT.52: Then the people began arguing with each other about what he meant. “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?” they asked.
NLT.53: So Jesus said again, “I tell you the truth, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you cannot have eternal life within you.
NLT.54: But anyone who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise that person at the last day.
NLT.55: For my flesh is true food, and my blood is true drink.
NLT.56: Anyone who eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in him.
NLT.57: I live because of the living Father who sent me; in the same way, anyone who feeds on me will live because of me.
NLT.58: I am the true bread that came down from heaven. Anyone who eats this bread will not die as your ancestors did (even though they ate the manna) but will live forever.”
NLT.59: He said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
NLT.60: Many of his disciples said, “This is very hard to understand. How can anyone accept it?”
NLT.61: Jesus was aware that his disciples were complaining, so he said to them, “Does this offend you?
NLT.62: Then what will you think if you see the Son of Man ascend to heaven again?
NLT.63: The Spirit alone gives eternal life. Human effort accomplishes nothing. And the very words I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
NLT.64: But some of you do not believe me.” (For Jesus knew from the beginning which ones didn’t believe, and he knew who would betray him.)
NLT.65: Then he said, “That is why I said that people can’t come to me unless the Father gives them to me.”
NLT.66: At this point many of his disciples turned away and deserted him.
NLT.67: Then Jesus turned to the Twelve and asked, “Are you also going to leave?”
NLT.68: Simon Peter replied, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words that give eternal life.
NLT.69: We believe, and we know you are the Holy One of God. ”
NLT.70: Then Jesus said, “I chose the twelve of you, but one is a devil.”
NLT.71: He was speaking of Judas, son of Simon Iscariot, one of the Twelve, who would later betray him.

WEB.1: After these things, Jesus went away to the other side of the sea of Galilee, which is also called the Sea of Tiberias.
WEB.2: A great multitude followed him, because they saw his signs which he did on those who were sick.
WEB.3: Jesus went up into the mountain, and he sat there with his disciples.
WEB.4: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was at hand.
WEB.5: Jesus therefore lifting up his eyes, and seeing that a great multitude was coming to him, said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread, that these may eat?”
WEB.6: This he said to test him, for he himself knew what he would do.
WEB.7: Philip answered him, “Two hundred denarii worth of bread is not sufficient for them, that everyone of them may receive a little.”
WEB.8: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,
WEB.9: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what are these among so many?”
WEB.10: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” Now there was much grass in that place. So the men sat down, in number about five thousand.
WEB.11: Jesus took the loaves; and having given thanks, he distributed to the disciples, and the disciples to those who were sitting down; likewise also of the fish as much as they desired.
WEB.12: When they were filled, he said to his disciples, “Gather up the broken pieces which are left over, that nothing be lost.”
WEB.13: So they gathered them up, and filled twelve baskets with broken pieces from the five barley loaves, which were left over by those who had eaten.
WEB.14: When therefore the people saw the sign which Jesus did, they said, “This is truly the prophet who comes into the world.”
WEB.15: Jesus therefore, perceiving that they were about to come and take him by force, to make him king, withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
WEB.16: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
WEB.17: and they entered into the boat, and were going over the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not come to them.
WEB.18: The sea was tossed by a great wind blowing.
WEB.19: When therefore they had rowed about twenty-five or thirty stadia,  they saw Jesus walking on the sea,  and drawing near to the boat; and they were afraid.
WEB.20: But he said to them, “It is I.  Don’t be afraid.”
WEB.21: They were willing therefore to receive him into the boat. Immediately the boat was at the land where they were going.
WEB.22: On the next day, the multitude that stood on the other side of the sea saw that there was no other boat there, except the one in which his disciples had embarked, and that Jesus hadn’t entered with his disciples into the boat, but his disciples had gone away alone.
WEB.23: However boats from Tiberias came near to the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
WEB.24: When the multitude therefore saw that Jesus wasn’t there, nor his disciples, they themselves got into the boats, and came to Capernaum, seeking Jesus.
WEB.25: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they asked him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
WEB.26: Jesus answered them, “Most certainly I tell you, you seek me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate of the loaves, and were filled.
WEB.27: Don’t work for the food which perishes, but for the food which remains to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give to you. For God the Father has sealed him.”
WEB.28: They said therefore to him, “What must we do, that we may work the works of God?”
WEB.29: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
WEB.30: They said therefore to him, “What then do you do for a sign, that we may see, and believe you? What work do you do?
WEB.31: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness. As it is written, ‘He gave them bread out of heaven  to eat.’”
WEB.32: Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly, I tell you, it wasn’t Moses who gave you the bread out of heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread out of heaven.
WEB.33: For the bread of God is that which comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world.”
WEB.34: They said therefore to him, “Lord, always give us this bread.”
WEB.35: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. He who comes to me will not be hungry, and he who believes in me will never be thirsty.
WEB.36: But I told you that you have seen me, and yet you don’t believe.
WEB.37: All those whom the Father gives me will come to me. He who comes to me I will in no way throw out.
WEB.38: For I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
WEB.39: This is the will of my Father who sent me, that of all he has given to me I should lose nothing, but should raise him up at the last day.
WEB.40: This is the will of the one who sent me, that everyone who sees the Son, and believes in him, should have eternal life; and I will raise him up at the last day.”
WEB.41: The Jews therefore murmured concerning him, because he said, “I am the bread which came down out of heaven.”
WEB.42: They said, “Isn’t this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How then does he say, ‘I have come down out of heaven?’”
WEB.43: Therefore Jesus answered them, “Don’t murmur among yourselves.
WEB.44: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up in the last day.
WEB.45: It is written in the prophets, ‘They will all be taught by God.’   Therefore everyone who hears from the Father, and has learned, comes to me.
WEB.46: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except he who is from God. He has seen the Father.
WEB.47: Most certainly, I tell you, he who believes in me has eternal life.
WEB.48: I am the bread of life.
WEB.49: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
WEB.50: This is the bread which comes down out of heaven, that anyone may eat of it and not die.
WEB.51: I am the living bread which came down out of heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. Yes, the bread which I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
WEB.52: The Jews therefore contended with one another, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
WEB.53: Jesus therefore said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you don’t have life in yourselves.
WEB.54: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.
WEB.55: For my flesh is food indeed, and my blood is drink indeed.
WEB.56: He who eats my flesh and drinks my blood lives in me, and I in him.
WEB.57: As the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father; so he who feeds on me, he will also live because of me.
WEB.58: This is the bread which came down out of heaven — not as our fathers ate the manna, and died. He who eats this bread will live forever.”
WEB.59: He said these things in the synagogue, as he taught in Capernaum.
WEB.60: Therefore many of his disciples, when they heard this, said, “This is a hard saying! Who can listen to it?”
WEB.61: But Jesus knowing in himself that his disciples murmured at this, said to them, “Does this cause you to stumble?
WEB.62: Then what if you would see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
WEB.63: It is the spirit who gives life. The flesh profits nothing. The words that I speak to you are spirit, and are life.
WEB.64: But there are some of you who don’t believe.” For Jesus knew from the beginning who they were who didn’t believe, and who it was who would betray him.
WEB.65: He said, “For this cause I have said to you that no one can come to me, unless it is given to him by my Father.”
WEB.66: At this, many of his disciples went back, and walked no more with him.
WEB.67: Jesus said therefore to the twelve, “You don’t also want to go away, do you?”
WEB.68: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
WEB.69: We have come to believe and know that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
WEB.70: Jesus answered them, “Didn’t I choose you, the twelve, and one of you is a devil?”
WEB.71: Now he spoke of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, for it was he who would betray him, being one of the twelve.
