CSB17.49: Your ancestors ate the manna <sup>ⓙ</sup>in the wilderness, <sup>ⓚ</sup>and they died.
CSB17.50: This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that anyone may eat of it and not die. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.51: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread he will live <sup>ⓜ</sup>forever. <sup>ⓝ</sup> The bread that I will give for the life <sup>ⓐ</sup>of the world is my flesh.” <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.52: At that, the Jews argued <sup>ⓒ</sup> among themselves, <sup>ⓓ</sup> “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
CSB17.53: So Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man <sup>ⓔ</sup>and drink his blood, <sup>ⓕ</sup>you do not have life in yourselves.
CSB17.54: The one who eats my flesh and drinks my blood <sup>ⓖ</sup>has eternal life, and I will raise <sup>ⓗ</sup>him up on the last day,
CSB17.55: because my flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
CSB17.56: The one who eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in him. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.57: Just as the living <sup>ⓙ</sup>Father sent me and I live because of the Father, so the one who feeds on me will live <sup>ⓚ</sup> because of me.
CSB17.58: This is the bread that came down from heaven; it is not like the manna <sup>[12]</sup> your ancestors ate — and they died. The one who eats this bread will live forever.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.59: He said these things while teaching in the synagogue <sup>ⓜ</sup> in Capernaum. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.60: Therefore, when many of his disciples heard this, they said, “This teaching is hard. Who can accept <sup>[13]</sup> it?”
CSB17.61: Jesus, knowing in himself <sup>ⓐ</sup> that his disciples were complaining about this, asked them, “Does this offend you?
CSB17.62: Then what if you were to observe the Son of Man <sup>ⓑ</sup> ascending <sup>ⓒ</sup>to where he was before?
CSB17.63: The Spirit <sup>ⓓ</sup>is the one who gives life. The flesh doesn’t help at all. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and are life.
CSB17.64: But there are some among you who don’t believe.” (For Jesus knew from the beginning <sup>ⓔ</sup> those who did not <sup>[14]</sup> believe and the one who would betray <sup>ⓕ</sup> him.)
CSB17.65: He said, “This is why I told you that no one can come to me <sup>ⓖ</sup>unless it is granted to him by the Father.”
CSB17.66: From that moment <sup>[15]</sup> many of his disciples turned back and no longer accompanied him.
CSB17.67: So Jesus said to the Twelve, <sup>ⓗ</sup> “You don’t want to go away too, do you?”
CSB17.68: Simon Peter <sup>ⓘ</sup> answered, “Lord, to whom will we go? You have the words of eternal life.
CSB17.69: We have come to believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.” <sup>[16]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.70: Jesus replied to them, “Didn’t I choose you, the Twelve? <sup>ⓚ</sup>Yet one of you is a devil.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.71: He was referring to Judas, <sup>ⓜ</sup> Simon Iscariot’s son, <sup>[17]</sup> one of the Twelve, because he was going to betray <sup>ⓝ</sup> him.

AMP.1: After this, Jesus went to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (or Sea of Tiberias).
AMP.2: A large crowd was following Him because they had seen the signs (attesting miracles) which He continually performed on those who were sick.
AMP.3: And Jesus went up on the mountainside and sat down there with His disciples.
AMP.4: Now the Passover, the feast of the Jews, was approaching.
AMP.5: Jesus looked up and saw that a large crowd was coming toward Him, and He said to Philip, "Where will we buy bread for these people to eat?"
AMP.6: But He said this to test Philip, because He knew what He was about to do.
AMP.7: Philip answered, "Two hundred denarii (200 days' wages) worth of bread is not enough for each one to receive even a little."
AMP.8: One of His disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter's brother, said to Him,
AMP.9: "There is a little boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish; but what are these for so many people?"
AMP.10: Jesus said, "Have the people sit down [to eat]." Now [the ground] there was [covered with] an abundance of grass, so the men sat down, about5,000 in number.
AMP.11: Then Jesus took the loaves, and when He had given thanks, He distributed them to those who were seated; the same also with the fish, as much as they wanted.
AMP.12: When they had eaten enough, He said to His disciples, "Gather up the leftover pieces so that nothing will be lost."
AMP.13: So they gathered them up, and they filled twelve large baskets with pieces from the five barley loaves which were left over by those who had eaten.
AMP.14: When the people saw the sign (attesting miracle) that He had done, they began saying, "This is without a doubt the promised Prophet who is to come into the world!"
AMP.15: Then Jesus, knowing that they were going to come and take Him by force to make Him king, withdrew again to the mountainside by Himself.
AMP.16: When evening came, His disciples went down to the sea,
AMP.17: and they got into a boat and started to cross the sea to Capernaum. It was already dark, and Jesus had still not come [back] to them.
AMP.18: The sea was getting rough and rising high because a strong wind was blowing.
AMP.19: Then, when they had rowed three or four miles [and were near the center of the sea], they saw Jesus walking on the sea and approaching the boat; and they were [terribly] frightened.
AMP.20: But Jesus said to them, "It is I (<e>I Am</e>); do not be afraid."
AMP.21: Then they were willing to take Him on board the boat, and immediately the boat reached the [shore of the] land to which they were going.
AMP.22: The next day the crowd that stood on the other side of the sea realized that there had been only one small boat there, and that Jesus had not boarded the boat with His disciples, but that His disciples had gone away alone.
AMP.23: [Now some] other small boats from Tiberias had come in near the place where they ate the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
AMP.24: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor His disciples were there, they boarded the small boats themselves and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
AMP.25: And when they found Him on the other side of the sea, they asked Him, "Rabbi, when did You get here?"
AMP.26: Jesus answered, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, you have been searching for Me, not because you saw the signs (attesting miracles), but because you ate the loaves and were filled.
AMP.27: Do not work for food that perishes, but for food that endures [and leads] to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you; for God the Father has authorized Him and put His seal on Him."
AMP.28: Then they asked Him, "What are we to do, so that we may habitually be doing the works of God?"
AMP.29: Jesus answered, "This is the work of God: that you believe [adhere to, trust in, rely on, and have faith] in the One whom He has sent."
AMP.30: So they said to Him, "What sign (attesting miracle) will You do that we may see it and believe You? What [supernatural] work will You do [as proof]?
AMP.31: Our fathers ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written [in Scripture], 'He gave them bread out of heaven to eat.'"
AMP.32: Then Jesus said to them, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, it is not Moses who has given you the bread out of heaven, but it is My Father who gives you the true bread out of heaven.
AMP.33: For the Bread of God is He who comes down out of heaven, and gives life to the world."
AMP.34: Then they said to Him, "Lord, always give us this bread."
AMP.35: Jesus replied to them, " I am the Bread of Life. The one who comes to Me will never be hungry, and the one who believes in Me [as Savior] will never be thirsty [for that one will be sustained spiritually].
AMP.36: But as I told you, you have seen Me and still you do not believe.
AMP.37: All that My Father gives Me will come to Me; and the one who comes to Me I will most certainly not cast out [I will never, never reject anyone who follows Me].
AMP.38: For I have come down from heaven, not to do My own will, but to do the will of Him who sent Me.
AMP.39: This is the will of Him who sent Me, that of all that He has given Me I lose nothing, but that I [give new life and] raise it up at the last day.
AMP.40: For this is My Father's will and purpose, that everyone who sees the Son and believes in Him [as Savior] will have eternal life, and I will raise him up [from the dead] on the last day."
AMP.41: Now the Jews murmured and found fault with Him because He said, "I am the Bread that came down out of heaven."
AMP.42: They kept saying, "Is this not Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How does He now [have the arrogance to] say, 'I have come down out of heaven'?"
AMP.43: So Jesus answered, "Stop murmuring among yourselves.
AMP.44: No one can come to Me unless the Father who sent Me draws him [giving him the desire to come to Me]; and I will raise him up [from the dead] on the last day.
AMP.45: It is written in the prophets, '<e>And they will all be taught of God</e>.' Everyone who has listened to and learned from the Father, comes to Me.
AMP.46: Not that anyone has seen the Father, except He [who was with the Father and] who is from God; He [alone] has seen the Father.
AMP.47: I assure you and most solemnly say to you, he who believes [in Me as Savior—whoever adheres to, trusts in, relies on, and has faith in Me—already] has eternal life [that is, now possesses it].
AMP.48: I am the Bread of Life [the Living Bread which gives and sustains life].
AMP.49: Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
AMP.50: This is the Bread that comes down out of heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
AMP.51: I am the Living Bread that came down out of heaven. If anyone eats of this Bread [believes in Me, accepts Me as Savior], he will live forever. And the Bread that I will give for the life of the world is My flesh (body)."
AMP.52: Then the Jews began to argue with one another, saying, "How can this man give us His flesh to eat?"
AMP.53: And Jesus said to them, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink His blood [unless you believe in Me as Savior and believe in the saving power of My blood which will be shed for you], you do not have life in yourselves.
AMP.54: The one who eats My flesh and drinks My blood [believes in Me, accepts Me as Savior] has eternal life [that is, now possesses it], and I will raise him up [from the dead] on the last day.
AMP.55: For My flesh is true [spiritual] food, and My blood is true [spiritual] drink.
AMP.56: He who eats My flesh and drinks My blood [believes in Me, accepts Me as Savior] remains in Me, and I [in the same way remain] in him.
AMP.57: Just as the living Father sent Me, and I live because of the Father, even so the one who feeds on Me [believes in Me, accepts Me as Savior] will also live because of Me.
AMP.58: This is the Bread which came down out of heaven. It is not like [the manna that] our fathers ate and they [eventually] died; the one who eats this Bread [believes in Me, accepts Me as Savior] will live forever."
AMP.59: He said these things in a synagogue while He was teaching in Capernaum.
AMP.60: When many of His disciples heard this, they said, "This is a difficult and harsh and offensive statement. Who can [be expected to] listen to it?"
AMP.61: But Jesus, aware that His disciples were complaining about it, asked them, "Does this cause you to stumble and take offense?
AMP.62: What then [will you think] if you see the Son of Man ascending to [the realm] where He was before?
AMP.63: It is the Spirit who gives life; the flesh conveys no benefit [it is of no account]. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and life [providing eternal life].
AMP.64: But [still] there are some of you who do not believe and have faith." For Jesus knew from the beginning who did not believe, and who would betray Him.
AMP.65: And He was saying, "This is the reason why I have told you that no one can come to Me unless it has been granted him [that is, unless he is enabled to do so] by the Father."
AMP.66: As a result of this many of His disciples abandoned Him, and no longer walked with Him.
AMP.67: So Jesus said to the twelve [disciples], "You do not want to leave too, do you?"
AMP.68: Simon Peter answered, "Lord, to whom shall we go? You [alone] have the words of eternal life [you are our only hope].
AMP.69: We have believed and confidently trusted, and [even more] we have come to know [by personal observation and experience] that You are the Holy One of God [the Christ, the Son of the living God]."
AMP.70: Jesus answered them, "Did I not choose you, the twelve [disciples]? And yet one of you is a devil (ally of Satan)."
AMP.71: Now He was speaking of Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot; for he, one of the twelve [disciples], was about to betray Him.

MSG.1: After this, Jesus went across the Sea of Galilee (some call it Tiberias).
MSG.2: A huge crowd followed him, attracted by the miracles they had seen him do among the sick.
MSG.3: When he got to the other side, he climbed a hill and sat down, surrounded by his disciples.
MSG.4: It was nearly time for the Feast of Passover, kept annually by the Jews.
MSG.5: When Jesus looked out and saw that a large crowd had arrived, he said to Philip, "Where can we buy bread to feed these people?"
MSG.6: He said this to stretch Philip's faith. He already knew what he was going to do.
MSG.7: Philip answered, "Two hundred silver pieces wouldn't be enough to buy bread for each person to get a piece."
MSG.8: One of the disciples—it was Andrew, brother to Simon Peter—said,
MSG.9: "There's a little boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish. But that's a drop in the bucket for a crowd like this."
MSG.10: Jesus said, "Make the people sit down." There was a nice carpet of green grass in this place. They sat down, about five thousand of them.
MSG.11: Then Jesus took the bread and, having given thanks, gave it to those who were seated. He did the same with the fish. All ate as much as they wanted.
MSG.12: When the people had eaten their fill, he said to his disciples, "Gather the leftovers so nothing is wasted."
MSG.13: They went to work and filled twelve large baskets with leftovers from the five barley loaves.
MSG.14: The people realized that God was at work among them in what Jesus had just done. They said, "This is the Prophet for sure, God's Prophet right here in Galilee!"
MSG.15: Jesus saw that in their enthusiasm, they were about to grab him and make him king, so he slipped off and went back up the mountain to be by himself.
MSG.16: In the evening his disciples went down to the sea,
MSG.17: got in the boat, and headed back across the water to Capernaum. It had grown quite dark and Jesus had not yet returned.
MSG.18: A huge wind blew up, churning the sea.
MSG.19: They were maybe three or four miles out when they saw Jesus walking on the sea, quite near the boat. They were scared senseless,
MSG.20: but he reassured them, "It's me. It's all right. Don't be afraid."
MSG.21: So they took him on board. In no time they reached land—the exact spot they were headed to.
MSG.22: The next day the crowd that was left behind realized that there had been only one boat, and that Jesus had not gotten into it with his disciples. They had seen them go off without him.
MSG.23: By now boats from Tiberias had pulled up near where they had eaten the bread blessed by the Master.
MSG.24: So when the crowd realized he was gone and wasn't coming back, they piled into the Tiberias boats and headed for Capernaum, looking for Jesus.
MSG.25: When they found him back across the sea, they said, "Rabbi, when did you get here?"
MSG.26: Jesus answered, "You've come looking for me not because you saw God in my actions but because I fed you, filled your stomachs—and for free.
MSG.27: "Don't waste your energy striving for perishable food like that. Work for the food that sticks with you, food that nourishes your lasting life, food the Son of Man provides. He and what he does are guaranteed by God the Father to last."
MSG.28: To that they said, "Well, what do we do then to get in on God's works?"
MSG.29: Jesus said, "Throw your lot in with the One that God has sent. That kind of a commitment gets you in on God's works."
MSG.30: They waffled: "Why don't you give us a clue about who you are, just a hint of what's going on? When we see what's up, we'll commit ourselves. Show us what you can do.
MSG.31: Moses fed our ancestors with bread in the desert. It says so in the Scriptures: 'He gave them bread from heaven to eat.'"
MSG.32: Jesus responded, "The real significance of that Scripture is not that Moses gave you bread from heaven but that my Father is right now offering you bread from heaven, the real bread.
MSG.33: The Bread of God came down out of heaven and is giving life to the world."
MSG.34: They jumped at that: "Master, give us this bread, now and forever!"
MSG.35: Jesus said, "I am the Bread of Life. The person who aligns with me hungers no more and thirsts no more, ever.
MSG.36: I have told you this explicitly because even though you have seen me in action, you don't really believe me.
MSG.37: Every person the Father gives me eventually comes running to me. And once that person is with me, I hold on and don't let go.
MSG.38: I came down from heaven not to follow my own whim but to accomplish the will of the One who sent me.
MSG.39: "This, in a nutshell, is that will: that everything handed over to me by the Father be completed—not a single detail missed—and at the wrap-up of time I have everything and everyone put together, upright and whole.
MSG.40: This is what my Father wants: that anyone who sees the Son and trusts who he is and what he does and then aligns with him will enter real life, eternal life. My part is to put them on their feet alive and whole at the completion of time."
MSG.41: At this, because he said, "I am the Bread that came down from heaven," the Jews started arguing over him:
MSG.42: "Isn't this the son of Joseph? Don't we know his father? Don't we know his mother? How can he now say, 'I came down out of heaven' and expect anyone to believe him?"
MSG.43: Jesus said, "Don't bicker among yourselves over me.
MSG.44: You're not in charge here. The Father who sent me is in charge. He draws people to me—that's the only way you'll ever come. Only then do I do my work, putting people together, setting them on their feet, ready for the End.
MSG.45: This is what the prophets meant when they wrote, 'And then they will all be personally taught by God.' Anyone who has spent any time at all listening to the Father, really listening and therefore learning, comes to me to be taught personally—to see it with his own eyes, hear it with his own ears, from me, since I have it firsthand from the Father.
MSG.46: No one has seen the Father except the One who has his Being alongside the Father—and you can see me.
MSG.47: "I'm telling you the most solemn and sober truth now: Whoever believes in me has real life, eternal life.
MSG.48: I am the Bread of Life.
MSG.49: Your ancestors ate the manna bread in the desert and died.
MSG.50: But now here is Bread that truly comes down out of heaven. Anyone eating this Bread will not die, ever.
MSG.51: I am the Bread—living Bread!—who came down out of heaven. Anyone who eats this Bread will live—and forever! The Bread that I present to the world so that it can eat and live is myself, this flesh-and-blood self."
MSG.52: At this, the Jews started fighting among themselves: "How can this man serve up his flesh for a meal?"
MSG.53: But Jesus didn't give an inch. "Only insofar as you eat and drink flesh and blood, the flesh and blood of the Son of Man, do you have life within you.
MSG.54: The one who brings a hearty appetite to this eating and drinking has eternal life and will be fit and ready for the Final Day.
MSG.55: My flesh is real food and my blood is real drink.
MSG.56: By eating my flesh and drinking my blood you enter into me and I into you.
MSG.57: In the same way that the fully alive Father sent me here and I live because of him, so the one who makes a meal of me lives because of me.
MSG.58: This is the Bread from heaven. Your ancestors ate bread and later died. Whoever eats this Bread will live always."
MSG.59: He said these things while teaching in the meeting place in Capernaum.
MSG.60: Many among his disciples heard this and said, "This is tough teaching, too tough to swallow."
MSG.61: Jesus sensed that his disciples were having a hard time with this and said, "Does this throw you completely?
MSG.62: What would happen if you saw the Son of Man ascending to where he came from?
MSG.63: The Spirit can make life. Sheer muscle and willpower don't make anything happen. Every word I've spoken to you is a Spirit-word, and so it is life-making.
MSG.64: But some of you are resisting, refusing to have any part in this." (Jesus knew from the start that some weren't going to risk themselves with him. He knew also who would betray him.)
MSG.65: He went on to say, "This is why I told you earlier that no one is capable of coming to me on his own. You get to me only as a gift from the Father."
MSG.66: After this a lot of his disciples left. They no longer wanted to be associated with him.
MSG.67: Then Jesus gave the Twelve their chance: "Do you also want to leave?"
MSG.68: Peter replied, "Master, to whom would we go? You have the words of real life, eternal life.
MSG.69: We've already committed ourselves, confident that you are the Holy One of God."
MSG.70: Jesus responded, "Haven't I handpicked you, the Twelve? Still, one of you is a devil!"
MSG.71: He was referring to Judas, son of Simon Iscariot. This man—one from the Twelve!—was even then getting ready to betray him.

NIV.1:
Jesus Feeds the Five Thousand
Some time after this, Jesus crossed to the far shore of the Sea of Galilee (that is, the Sea of Tiberias),
NIV.2: and a great crowd of people followed him because they saw the miraculous signs he had performed on the sick.
NIV.3: Then Jesus went up on a mountainside and sat down with his disciples.
NIV.4: The Jewish Passover Feast was near.
NIV.5: When Jesus looked up and saw a great crowd coming toward him, he said to Philip, “Where shall we buy bread for these people to eat?”
NIV.6: He asked this only to test him, for he already had in mind what he was going to do.
NIV.7: Philip answered him, “Eight months’ wages would not buy enough bread for each one to have a bite!”
NIV.8: Another of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, spoke up,
NIV.9: “Here is a boy with five small barley loaves and two small fish, but how far will they go among so many?”
NIV.10: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” There was plenty of grass in that place, and the men sat down, about five thousand of them.
NIV.11: Jesus then took the loaves, gave thanks, and distributed to those who were seated as much as they wanted. He did the same with the fish.
NIV.12: When they had all had enough to eat, he said to his disciples, “Gather the pieces that are left over. Let nothing be wasted.”
NIV.13: So they gathered them and filled twelve baskets with the pieces of the five barley loaves left over by those who had eaten.
NIV.14: After the people saw the miraculous sign that Jesus did, they began to say, “Surely this is the Prophet who is to come into the world.”
NIV.15: Jesus, knowing that they intended to come and make him king by force, withdrew again to a mountain by himself.
NIV.16:
Jesus Walks on the Water
When evening came, his disciples went down to the lake,
NIV.17: where they got into a boat and set off across the lake for Capernaum. By now it was dark, and Jesus had not yet joined them.
NIV.18: A strong wind was blowing and the waters grew rough.
NIV.19: When they had rowed three or three and a half miles, they saw Jesus approaching the boat, walking on the water; and they were terrified.
NIV.20: But he said to them, “It is I; don’t be afraid.”
NIV.21: Then they were willing to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat reached the shore where they were heading.
NIV.22: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the opposite shore of the lake realized that only one boat had been there, and that Jesus had not entered it with his disciples, but that they had gone away alone.
NIV.23: Then some boats from Tiberias landed near the place where the people had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
NIV.24: Once the crowd realized that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and went to Capernaum in search of Jesus.
NIV.25:
Jesus the Bread of Life
When they found him on the other side of the lake, they asked him, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
NIV.26: Jesus answered, “I tell you the truth, you are looking for me, not because you saw miraculous signs but because you ate the loaves and had your fill.
NIV.27: Do not work for food that spoils, but for food that endures to eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. On him God the Father has placed his seal of approval.”
NIV.28: Then they asked him, “What must we do to do the works God requires?”
NIV.29: Jesus answered, “The work of God is this: to believe in the one he has sent.”
NIV.30: So they asked him, “What miraculous sign then will you give that we may see it and believe you? What will you do?
NIV.31: Our forefathers ate the manna in the desert; as it is written: ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
NIV.32: Jesus said to them, “I tell you the truth, it is not Moses who has given you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.
NIV.33: For the bread of God is he who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
NIV.34: “Sir,” they said, “from now on give us this bread.”
NIV.35: Then Jesus declared, “I am the bread of life. He who comes to me will never go hungry, and he who believes in me will never be thirsty.
NIV.36: But as I told you, you have seen me and still you do not believe.
NIV.37: All that the Father gives me will come to me, and whoever comes to me I will never drive away.
NIV.38: For I have come down from heaven not to do my will but to do the will of him who sent me.
NIV.39: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I shall lose none of all that he has given me, but raise them up at the last day.
NIV.40: For my Father’s will is that everyone who looks to the Son and believes in him shall have eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.”
NIV.41: At this the Jews began to grumble about him because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”
NIV.42: They said, “Is this not Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How can he now say, ‘I came down from heaven’?”
NIV.44: “No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up at the last day.
NIV.45: It is written in the Prophets: ‘They will all be taught by God.’ Everyone who listens to the Father and learns from him comes to me.
NIV.46: No one has seen the Father except the one who is from God; only he has seen the Father.
NIV.47: I tell you the truth, he who believes has everlasting life.
NIV.48: I am the bread of life.
NIV.49: Your forefathers ate the manna in the desert, yet they died.
NIV.50: But here is the bread that comes down from heaven, which a man may eat and not die.
NIV.51: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread, he will live forever. This bread is my flesh, which I will give for the life of the world.”
NIV.52: Then the Jews began to argue sharply among themselves, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
NIV.53: Jesus said to them, “I tell you the truth, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
NIV.54: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.
NIV.55: For my flesh is real food and my blood is real drink.
NIV.56: Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in him.
NIV.57: Just as the living Father sent me and I live because of the Father, so the one who feeds on me will live because of me.
NIV.58: This is the bread that came down from heaven. Your forefathers ate manna and died, but he who feeds on this bread will live forever.”
NIV.59: He said this while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
NIV.60:
Many Disciples Desert Jesus
On hearing it, many of his disciples said, “This is a hard teaching. Who can accept it?”
NIV.61: Aware that his disciples were grumbling about this, Jesus said to them, “Does this offend you?
NIV.62: What if you see the Son of Man ascend to where he was before!
NIV.63: The Spirit gives life; the flesh counts for nothing. The words I have spoken to you are spirit and they are life.
NIV.64: Yet there are some of you who do not believe.” For Jesus had known from the beginning which of them did not believe and who would betray him.
NIV.65: He went on to say, “This is why I told you that no one can come to me unless the Father has enabled him.”
NIV.66: From this time many of his disciples turned back and no longer followed him.
NIV.67: “You do not want to leave too, do you?” Jesus asked the Twelve.
NIV.68: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom shall we go? You have the words of eternal life.
NIV.69: We believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.”
NIV.70: Then Jesus replied, “Have I not chosen you, the Twelve? Yet one of you is a devil!”
NIV.71: (He meant Judas, the son of Simon Iscariot, who, though one of the Twelve, was later to betray him.)

NRSVCE.1: After this Jesus went to the other side of the Sea of Galilee, also called the Sea of Tiberias.
NRSVCE.2: A large crowd kept following him, because they saw the signs that he was doing for the sick.
NRSVCE.3: Jesus went up the mountain and sat down there with his disciples.
NRSVCE.4: Now the Passover, the festival of the Jews, was near.
NRSVCE.5: When he looked up and saw a large crowd coming toward him, Jesus said to Philip, “Where are we to buy bread for these people to eat?”
NRSVCE.6: He said this to test him, for he himself knew what he was going to do.
NRSVCE.7: Philip answered him, “Six months’ wages would not buy enough bread for each of them to get a little.”
NRSVCE.8: One of his disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,
NRSVCE.9: “There is a boy here who has five barley loaves and two fish. But what are they among so many people?”
NRSVCE.10: Jesus said, “Make the people sit down.” Now there was a great deal of grass in the place; so they sat down, about five thousand in all.
NRSVCE.11: Then Jesus took the loaves, and when he had given thanks, he distributed them to those who were seated; so also the fish, as much as they wanted.
NRSVCE.12: When they were satisfied, he told his disciples, “Gather up the fragments left over, so that nothing may be lost.”
NRSVCE.13: So they gathered them up, and from the fragments of the five barley loaves, left by those who had eaten, they filled twelve baskets.
NRSVCE.14: When the people saw the sign that he had done, they began to say, “This is indeed the prophet who is to come into the world.”
NRSVCE.15: When Jesus realized that they were about to come and take him by force to make him king, he withdrew again to the mountain by himself.
NRSVCE.16: When evening came, his disciples went down to the sea,
NRSVCE.17: got into a boat, and started across the sea to Capernaum. It was now dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.
NRSVCE.18: The sea became rough because a strong wind was blowing.
NRSVCE.19: When they had rowed about three or four miles, they saw Jesus walking on the sea and coming near the boat, and they were terrified.
NRSVCE.20: But he said to them, “It is I; do not be afraid.”
NRSVCE.21: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat reached the land toward which they were going.
NRSVCE.22: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the other side of the sea saw that there had been only one boat there. They also saw that Jesus had not got into the boat with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
NRSVCE.23: Then some boats from Tiberias came near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
NRSVCE.24: So when the crowd saw that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they themselves got into the boats and went to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
NRSVCE.25: When they found him on the other side of the sea, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you come here?”
NRSVCE.26: Jesus answered them, “Very truly, I tell you, you are looking for me, not because you saw signs, but because you ate your fill of the loaves.
NRSVCE.27: Do not work for the food that perishes, but for the food that endures for eternal life, which the Son of Man will give you. For it is on him that God the Father has set his seal.”
NRSVCE.28: Then they said to him, “What must we do to perform the works of God?”
NRSVCE.29: Jesus answered them, “This is the work of God, that you believe in him whom he has sent.”
NRSVCE.30: So they said to him, “What sign are you going to give us then, so that we may see it and believe you? What work are you performing?
NRSVCE.31: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness; as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
NRSVCE.32: Then Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but it is my Father who gives you the true bread from heaven.
NRSVCE.33: For the bread of God is that which comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
NRSVCE.34: They said to him, “Sir, give us this bread always.”
NRSVCE.35: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never be hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.
NRSVCE.36: But I said to you that you have seen me and yet do not believe.
NRSVCE.37: Everything that the Father gives me will come to me, and anyone who comes to me I will never drive away;
NRSVCE.38: for I have come down from heaven, not to do my own will, but the will of him who sent me.
NRSVCE.39: And this is the will of him who sent me, that I should lose nothing of all that he has given me, but raise it up on the last day.
NRSVCE.40: This is indeed the will of my Father, that all who see the Son and believe in him may have eternal life; and I will raise them up on the last day.”
NRSVCE.41: Then the Jews began to complain about him because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven.”
NRSVCE.42: They were saying, “Is not this Jesus, the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How can he now say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
NRSVCE.43: Jesus answered them, “Do not complain among yourselves.
NRSVCE.44: No one can come to me unless drawn by the Father who sent me; and I will raise that person up on the last day.
NRSVCE.45: It is written in the prophets, ‘And they shall all be taught by God.’ Everyone who has heard and learned from the Father comes to me.
NRSVCE.46: Not that anyone has seen the Father except the one who is from God; he has seen the Father.
NRSVCE.47: Very truly, I tell you, whoever believes has eternal life.
NRSVCE.48: I am the bread of life.
NRSVCE.49: Your ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
NRSVCE.50: This is the bread that comes down from heaven, so that one may eat of it and not die.
NRSVCE.51: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. Whoever eats of this bread will live forever; and the bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
NRSVCE.52: The Jews then disputed among themselves, saying, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
NRSVCE.53: So Jesus said to them, “Very truly, I tell you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you.
NRSVCE.54: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood have eternal life, and I will raise them up on the last day;
NRSVCE.55: for my flesh is true food and my blood is true drink.
NRSVCE.56: Those who eat my flesh and drink my blood abide in me, and I in them.
NRSVCE.57: Just as the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so whoever eats me will live because of me.
NRSVCE.58: This is the bread that came down from heaven, not like that which your ancestors ate, and they died. But the one who eats this bread will live forever.”
NRSVCE.59: He said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue at Capernaum.
NRSVCE.60: When many of his disciples heard it, they said, “This teaching is difficult; who can accept it?”
NRSVCE.61: But Jesus, being aware that his disciples were complaining about it, said to them, “Does this offend you?
NRSVCE.62: Then what if you were to see the Son of Man ascending to where he was before?
NRSVCE.63: It is the spirit that gives life; the flesh is useless. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and life.
NRSVCE.64: But among you there are some who do not believe.” For Jesus knew from the first who were the ones that did not believe, and who was the one that would betray him.
NRSVCE.65: And he said, “For this reason I have told you that no one can come to me unless it is granted by the Father.”
NRSVCE.66: Because of this many of his disciples turned back and no longer went about with him.
NRSVCE.67: So Jesus asked the twelve, “Do you also wish to go away?”
NRSVCE.68: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom can we go? You have the words of eternal life.
NRSVCE.69: We have come to believe and know that you are the Holy One of God.”
NRSVCE.70: Jesus answered them, “Did I not choose you, the twelve? Yet one of you is a devil.”
NRSVCE.71: He was speaking of Judas son of Simon Iscariot, for he, though one of the twelve, was going to betray him.

NET.1: After this Jesus went away to the other side of the Sea of Galilee (also called the Sea of Tiberias).
NET.2: A large crowd was following him because they were observing the miraculous signs he was performing on the sick.
NET.3: So Jesus went on up the mountainside and sat down there with his disciples.
NET.4: (Now the Jewish feast of the Passover was near.)
NET.5: Then Jesus, when he looked up and saw that a large crowd was coming to him, said to Philip, “Where can we buy bread so that these people may eat?”
NET.6: (Now Jesus said this to test him, for he knew what he was going to do.)
NET.7: Philip replied, “Two hundred silver coins worth of bread would not be enough for them, for each one to get a little.”
NET.8: One of Jesus’ disciples, Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother, said to him,
NET.9: “Here is a boy who has five barley loaves and two fish, but what good are these for so many people?”
NET.10: Jesus said, “Have the people sit down.” (Now there was a lot of grass in that place.) So the men sat down, about five thousand in number.
NET.11: Then Jesus took the loaves, and when he had given thanks, he distributed the bread to those who were seated. He then did the same with the fish, as much as they wanted.
NET.12: When they were all satisfied, Jesus said to his disciples, “Gather up the broken pieces that are left over, so that nothing is wasted.”
NET.13: So they gathered them up and filled twelve baskets with broken pieces from the five barley loaves left over by the people who had eaten.
NET.14: Now when the people saw the miraculous sign that Jesus performed, they began to say to one another, “This is certainly the Prophet who is to come into the world.”
NET.15: Then Jesus, because he knew they were going to come and seize him by force to make him king, withdrew again up the mountainside alone.
NET.16: Now when evening came, his disciples went down to the lake,
NET.17: got into a boat, and started to cross the lake to Capernaum. (It had already become dark, and Jesus had not yet come to them.)
NET.18: By now a strong wind was blowing and the sea was getting rough.
NET.19: Then, when they had rowed about three or four miles, they caught sight of Jesus walking on the lake, approaching the boat, and they were frightened.
NET.20: But he said to them, “It is I. Do not be afraid.”
NET.21: Then they wanted to take him into the boat, and immediately the boat came to the land where they had been heading.
NET.22: The next day the crowd that remained on the other side of the lake realized that only one small boat had been there, and that Jesus had not boarded it with his disciples, but that his disciples had gone away alone.
NET.23: But some boats from Tiberias came to shore near the place where they had eaten the bread after the Lord had given thanks.
NET.24: So when the crowd realized that neither Jesus nor his disciples were there, they got into the boats and came to Capernaum looking for Jesus.
NET.25: When they found him on the other side of the lake, they said to him, “Rabbi, when did you get here?”
NET.26: Jesus replied, “I tell you the solemn truth, you are looking for me not because you saw miraculous signs, but because you ate all the loaves of bread you wanted.
NET.27: Do not work for the food that disappears, but for the food that remains to eternal life — the food which the Son of Man will give to you. For God the Father has put his seal of approval on him.”
NET.28: So then they said to him, “What must we do to accomplish the deeds God requires?”
NET.29: Jesus replied, “This is the deed God requires — to believe in the one whom he sent.”
NET.30: So they said to him, “Then what miraculous sign will you perform, so that we may see it and believe you? What will you do?
NET.31: Our ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, just as it is written, ‘He gave them bread from heaven to eat.’”
NET.32: Then Jesus told them, “I tell you the solemn truth, it is not Moses who has given you the bread from heaven, but my Father is giving you the true bread from heaven.
NET.33: For the bread of God is the one who comes down from heaven and gives life to the world.”
NET.34: So they said to him, “Sir, give us this bread all the time!”
NET.35: Jesus said to them, “I am the bread of life. The one who comes to me will never go hungry, and the one who believes in me will never be thirsty.
NET.36: But I told you that you have seen me and still do not believe.
NET.37: Everyone whom the Father gives me will come to me, and the one who comes to me I will never send away.
NET.38: For I have come down from heaven not to do my own will but the will of the one who sent me.
NET.39: Now this is the will of the one who sent me — that I should not lose one person of every one he has given me, but raise them all up at the last day.
NET.40: For this is the will of my Father — for everyone who looks on the Son and believes in him to have eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day.”
NET.41: Then the Jews who were hostile to Jesus began complaining about him because he said, “I am the bread that came down from heaven,”
NET.42: and they said, “Isn’t this Jesus the son of Joseph, whose father and mother we know? How can he now say, ‘I have come down from heaven’?”
NET.43: Jesus replied, “Do not complain about me to one another.
NET.44: No one can come to me unless the Father who sent me draws him, and I will raise him up at the last day.
NET.45: It is written in the prophets, ‘And they will all be taught by God.’ Everyone who hears and learns from the Father comes to me.
NET.46: (Not that anyone has seen the Father except the one who is from God — he has seen the Father.)
NET.47: I tell you the solemn truth, the one who believes has eternal life.
NET.48: I am the bread of life.
NET.49: Your ancestors ate the manna in the wilderness, and they died.
NET.50: This is the bread that has come down from heaven, so that a person may eat from it and not die.
NET.51: I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats from this bread he will live forever. The bread that I will give for the life of the world is my flesh.”
NET.52: Then the Jews who were hostile to Jesus began to argue with one another, “How can this man give us his flesh to eat?”
NET.53: Jesus said to them, “I tell you the solemn truth, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in yourselves.
NET.54: The one who eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up on the last day.
NET.55: For my flesh is true food, and my blood is true drink.
NET.56: The one who eats my flesh and drinks my blood resides in me, and I in him.
NET.57: Just as the living Father sent me, and I live because of the Father, so the one who consumes me will live because of me.
NET.58: This is the bread that came down from heaven; it is not like the bread your ancestors ate, but then later died. The one who eats this bread will live forever.”
NET.59: Jesus said these things while he was teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
NET.60: Then many of his disciples, when they heard these things, said, “This is a difficult saying! Who can understand it?”
NET.61: When Jesus was aware that his disciples were complaining about this, he said to them, “Does this cause you to be offended?
NET.62: Then what if you see the Son of Man ascending where he was before?
NET.63: The Spirit is the one who gives life; human nature is of no help! The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and are life.
NET.64: But there are some of you who do not believe.” (For Jesus had already known from the beginning who those were who did not believe, and who it was who would betray him.)
NET.65: So Jesus added, “Because of this I told you that no one can come to me unless the Father has allowed him to come.”
NET.66: After this many of his disciples quit following him and did not accompany him any longer.
NET.67: So Jesus said to the twelve, “You don’t want to go away too, do you?”
NET.68: Simon Peter answered him, “Lord, to whom would we go? You have the words of eternal life.
NET.69: We have come to believe and to know that you are the Holy One of God!”
NET.70: Jesus replied, “Didn’t I choose you, the twelve, and yet one of you is the devil?”
NET.71: (Now he said this about Judas son of Simon Iscariot, for Judas, one of the twelve, was going to betray him.)
